zotero.abstractNote: The excitability of the human cortex fluctuates over time, influencing how we perceive and respond to sensory stimuli. While non-invasive brain stimulation can probe these fluctuations, real-time tracking of cortical excitability from electroencephalography (EEG) remains challenging due to the high dimensionality and non-stationarity of EEG signals. Here, we present a framework for the personalized, real-time inference of momentary cortical excitability from human EEG. We combine a biophysical model of cortical excitability with a deep learning-based inference engine to estimate excitability states from short segments of resting-state EEG. We validate our approach using concurrent transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) and EEG, demonstrating that our model can predict the amplitude of motor-evoked potentials (MEPs) in real-time. Our framework provides a robust and flexible tool for closed-loop brain stimulation, enabling the targeting of specific excitability states to modulate neural processing and behavior.
